title: Deaths from drowning
caption:
Deaths from drowning

This choropleth world map displays the estimated number of deaths from drowning in 2023. The legend uses a color scale ranging from pale yellow for values near zero to dark blue for values approaching 300,000, with specific thresholds marked at 300, 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, 30,000, and 100,000. The darkest blue shades, indicating the highest absolute numbers of deaths, are concentrated in South and East Asia. China and India clearly fall into the highest visible bands, with values exceeding 100,000 and approaching the 300,000 upper limit of the scale. Surrounding countries in Southeast Asia, such as Indonesia and Bangladesh, appear in medium-to-dark blue shades that sit between the 10,000 and 30,000 thresholds. In contrast, the United States, Brazil, and Russia are depicted in mid-range teal shades, placing them in the 3,000 to 10,000 range. Much of Africa, the Middle East, and Western Europe is covered in lighter green and yellow-green hues, corresponding to values generally below 3,000. Australia, Canada, and several nations in South America appear in the lightest yellow shades, indicating figures near the bottom of the scale. Greenland and a few other small territories are marked with a hatched pattern, signifying that no data is available for those regions. The visual distribution highlights a significant concentration of drowning deaths in the most populous Asian nations, while lower absolute numbers are observed across the Americas, Europe, and Oceania.
